text.title: Prompt Basics — Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every PromptTemplate must enforce. Source: OpenAI Prompt Engineering guide, Anthropic prompt design guidance, internal regression logs.

rule.statement: Prompts live as code constants (or YAML files committed to the repo). No runtime string concatenation, no database-fetched prompts in production code paths.
rule.rationale: Runtime-constructed prompts evade code review and are invisible in git diff; regressions surface only after model version rollover.

rule.statement: Few-shot examples capped at 3–5 high-quality pairs. The most representative example is placed last. Adding more examples without measured lift is rejected.

rationale.source: OpenAI Prompt Engineering guide; attention decay studies

rule.rationale: Model attention to early examples decays; more examples inflate tokens without reliable quality lift.

rule.statement: Every prompt names the output format explicitly: "Return JSON matching this shape: …", "Reply with one word from {positive, negative, neutral}", "Output only the SQL query, no commentary".
rule.rationale: Without an explicit format instruction the model picks one; outputs become unparseable for downstream stages.

rule.statement: User-supplied fields are inserted via `user_template.format(**user_inputs)` with `{var}` placeholders only — never f-strings concatenated at call time, never raw user text injected into the system role.
rule.rationale: Concatenating user text into system content lets the user override constraints; placeholder rendering keeps user input in the user role only.

rule.statement: Every PromptTemplate has a non-empty `system` field that names the role, constraints, and output format. No template ships with `system=""`.
rule.rationale: The default behaviour without a system prompt is wildly variable across model versions; an explicit system role pins behaviour.
